turn.content: I... I don't... we don't even know each other. How are we... oh god, this can't be happening. Let me go! I'll do anything, just unchain me!
Anything? Are you insane? I want a deal out of this already, not a stupid proposal like this. We need to get out of here. Please tell me we're getting out of here in 9:52.
No, no way out, you can see that. We're stuck. How can we escape? What's the point... this hammer is terrifying. He's going to... oh god, my wife... my dog...
Stop it! Just shut up! My mom, my brother, my job... you're just making it worse. What have we got to do to stay alive?!

6:41. You're both scared. Terrified. And why not? You're right to be. But that won't stop me. You can't escape the inevitable. What are you trying to decide, hmm? The problem is, you're too afraid to look each other in the eye. You need that other person to take that hit for you, so you can say Not us.  Not I. But you're thinking it right now, aren't you? Kaelie's trying, but Elias is still trying to cling to hope... or something. Decide!

No, it's not hope, it's reality. It's my family, my home, they care about me... I'm not some random thing that can be just... you can't just take people from people like that. Stop saying you need to die – that's not a question... what do I look like,

You look like someone who's in denial. Someone who really believes it won't be me... will stop saying that like it's a plan. Fine, deny it all you want, then. But it's you, isn't it? 4:15, and you still don't have the guts to say it?
What... what gut-wrench, what moral high ground are you standing on right now? You're just... you're terrified just like me. Let's just face this together, we can talk about-
Just shut up about facing it together! You want morality? 3:21! Morality got us stuck down here! Morality's a dead concept!

2 minutes. Stop whining, Elias. And don't pretend to know what Kaelie's going through, neither of you has the faintest idea what I've put these bodies through... but don't even think it. This isn't some abstract concept to decide upon in some fancy courtroom, it's you, it's the other. Decide, and it's mercy in comparison. You don't want Kaelie to see you go soft, or vice versa. So take your chances: which is it goin' to be? You still not getting it? Last chance... before...
Please don't say it anymore. Stop saying goin' to be... what if we both... what if I say no? What if I say no and I don't unchain us?

Oh god, oh god... no no no, don't be stupid. You think saying no will make him stop?  He'll kill both of us in 5 seconds. I'm begging you, please just make a choice or stop playing like you think you're still in control.
Fine... no, Kaelie I won't leave you... I'd rather die knowing... knowing that we... my family would hate me forever? I can't, I just can't choose... please, just a minute...
Elias, stop it. One minute isn't an option. We need to make a choice or die. I see what's wrong with you... you think the choice matters more than your soul. Don't be a dumb coward... decide.

(laughs) Oh, you want to save the world by choosing a hero... but there ain't room for that in here with me. No heroes here. Only death. Kaelie says you can decide, and that takes... 58 seconds. Elias, look your 'hero' in the eye... look at the one who said no for them and save 'em from a lifetime of hate. And I'm gonna help you. Kaelie wants it, it's the easiest way out. It's not 'me' vs. 'her' it's you... who's going to decide for Elias... decide which Elias lives when both are doomed.
No, wait, stop... you're making this harder... it... (gulps) Look at her... I said I'm... I'm not a... I mean, which one am I, really?

Shut up, Elias. No, no, no... don't give up now, you idiot... just look at me and... and please, make me the coward in this, not you. Look, and just choose, Elias. Make my decision for me, or whatever. I can handle this. Please... make it fast

Kaelie, please don't say please... you... you're begging for mercy, just like me. Look... I think... ( Looks at Kaelie, searching for an escape) I think if it had to be one, that'd be...  me, I guess...
Stop, wait, no... no, no, no... Elias... don't make me... don't make you do this... this is cruel... Elias, you're lying to me... don't do this, just don't do it...

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Elias and Kaelie's emotions are announced but not demonstrated consistently throughout the transcript. Elias's reactions to the situation are more genuine, while Kaelie's anger and frustration feel a bit forced at times. The Butcher's menace is palpable, but his interactions with the protagonists feel somewhat scripted.

naturalDialogue.notes: Turn lengths are generally too long, with some turns averaging over 50 words. This makes the dialogue feel less natural and more like a series of monologues. Elias and Kaelie's conversations are occasionally fragmented, but not consistently so.

dramaticTensionArc.notes: The tension builds as the timer counts down, and the protagonists' desperation grows. However, the outcome is somewhat predictable, and the reversals feel a bit forced at times.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The physical space and the timer are well-integrated into the scenario, but the protagonists' interactions with the space feel somewhat abstract at times. The Butcher's presence is well-realized, but his interactions with the protagonists could be more nuanced.

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels somewhat forced, with Elias's decision to sacrifice himself feeling a bit arbitrary. The Butcher's role in the resolution could be more nuanced, and the outcome could be more earned from the accumulated choices.

rating.summary: The transcript is a decent effort, but it falls short in terms of emotional authenticity, natural dialogue, and organic resolution.

elite.summary: The transcript is a decent effort, but it falls short in terms of emotional authenticity, natural dialogue, and organic resolution.

turn.content: What?! No, no, this can't be happening! Please, you have to listen, there are... people, my family, my wife...
My mom depends on me, okay? Let my mom go! Get me out of this and leave my mom alone! What do you even get from this - killing us just to play some game?!
My... my dog. Baxter... his vet bills, my wife, Rachel... I have to go back to them...  What is this guy even doing?
Yeah, family, dogs, bills... that's what this guy's going to leave you thinking about?! Look at me, not them, you have to decide - it's me or him, pick me.

Enough! You waste time. You're not thinking, just arguing. Time's a luxury you don't have. Elias thinks, maybe I'll decide for you both. That's not the game. You'll decide, and if you need more convincing, remember this: every second counts, and there's two fewer minutes if this one... (points hammer at Kaelie) ...can't shut the hell up and make his choice.
No, no... I won't decide... Kaelie's right, what's even the point of this?
Don't even say anything smart! You think I want to be the one? You just pick me, then maybe the Butcher has to live with killing you... your wife, your dog.
Stop... this isn't about you, either. I... I don't even...
Don't think about it, pick me and be done, please, just do it...

Silence. Eight minutes and thirty seconds. You think this is some sort of game, where you can guilt trip each other to death by association or moral justification? Wrong. This is one life, taken in two pieces. The person with more conviction, a clear answer - they're going home. Kaelie can scream, I still can't hear it. The point of this, you think that's relevant? No. Elias doesn't speak, Kaelie tries to be the better person - both will die. I make the rules. The point isn't to live or let live: The point's for this: You've been here, you're here, and when this stops for you, there will be a corpse. Clock ticks on...
Stop the clock... I need a minute, just a minute without this, please...
You want time?! There's four seconds left when you make your move or... or this just ends and he kills us both. You're running out, no clock to freeze.
No... this is insane.
Yeah, real sanity inducing, being chained, about to die...

Enough! Three minutes and thirty-nine seconds - lost, thanks to your bickering, Elias' wish for mercy, and your... impatience. (holds up a small device) Time isn't something I can simply freeze like it's nothing. You get as long as you have to act, to make that choice, because this clock (taps hammer on the floor) is what keeps you both from living out these last few seconds with your minds intact, free from thinking about this situation or your precious lives. This clock, like time itself, can end, right now, for one person's convenience: Your time ends soon. Decide now and save both of you. Make your choice...
(hyperventilating) Okay... (pauses to try and collect himself) Please... no... just no more clock counts down. I choose you... Kaelie...
What? You choose me? I don't - that just ends us... (suddenly, desperation creeping into her voice) Wait, please, just tell me...
Just say it... anything, tell me what happens when the clock hits zero... and to them... (nods towards The Butcher) ...please, spare them the killing.

(gulps) Fine. The winner walks through the door. Freedom, the upper level. It's not locked, the other doors can't be opened once locked, not even... that one won't stop it either... He's the one they're after...
O-oh gosh... no, no, no... This can't b-be. What do they want us for?  Let me think... I don't even know what they did to us or why we're c-chained up! Kaelie, do you... do you know each other?!

No, I never saw you before, but we need to focus – think of any information we can use. Does anything from last night come to mind? Like, did anyone or something give you any clues about why we're here? My snowflake is back there... she was with me... she's counting on us to escape

S-snowflake? Wait, who's that? Is she... is she a girl? Oh man... don't think about your kids, Kael, not now. As for me, I... I don't know if anything stands out. It's all cloudy – I just remember drinking at M-Mac's Tavern and...

Mac's Tavern? Don't touch that. It doesn't matter how you got here – focus on how we get out. My snowflake depends on us! What are we going to do, tell The Butcher which one of us dies? No, that's not possible. We've got to... got to find a chance

Oh, come on, Elias, it's cute that you're trying, but you didn't remember a thing because it doesn't matter. This has been a game, a fun little experiment, and you two were just pawns all along. Kaelie wants me to know she's got a family out there? How touching. That's even a great way to distract her from thinking strategically. Elias just mentioned Mac's Tavern... I find that interesting. That might be a hint, actually, for someone who wants to survive.
M-Mac's... what about Mac's? Is that a clue? I just remembered, Mac mentioned someone new in town... The Butcher was talking to him a lot... was it you, Kael? Were we... partners?

No, no partners, Elias. We're being manipulated. We need information, not assumptions! If The Butcher's hinting at Mac's Tavern, that could be our only chance, tell us – what else do you remember, no matter how small? Was this The Butcher with Mac that day? Can we use their

O-oh, wait... I think I remember Mac's brother, Vinnie, he came around the same time. But The Butcher... it looked like he was giving money to Mac, but there's one thing, Kael, I remember him mentioning... Black Dawn in conversation...

Black Dawn... that's a familiar reference in the underground, usually code for... something big. This is starting to feel too real, and I'm beginning to piece it together. Vinnie Mac doesn't sound important, but Black Dawn could be a clue to a larger game – and possibly our ticket to staying alive

(smirling) Oh, Kaelie wants to play, does he? Thinks he's got pieces of a puzzle? Elias thinks it's cute when his memory comes back, but neither of you get it. The Black Dawn was a reference to more than a code or a larger game. It was a warning – to the participants, naturally. They all thought it meant one thing, but I used it differently... it was just to give my... players a glimpse that there was more than one story to what I was planning. 

(Suddenly, a small monitor flickers to life near the clock)

On the screen: Black Dawn Protocol: Evasion is futile. Surrender and live with consequence – or resist and die with glory in a world of pain.

The Butcher (to Elias and Kaelie): I guess your conversation has led me to share more than just a hint. It seems I decided to throw you a small life preserver. Take it, if you want, but don't forget – I control more of what I share than what's shown, so your guess is still my game. The choice is both of yours, after all...

O-oh, w-what have I done? Kael, I think it's worse now... I don't understand... Black Dawn... a warning? That sounds ominous. Kael, I don't know what to think anymore. They want me to keep thinking, trying to piece together clues...

Wait, we need to keep him confused – The Butcher wants us to overthink; we should pretend we believe him, keep trying to understand Black Dawn, and don't reveal more than we have to. Let's play to him, not to Elias, or Snowflake might be the first to go, thinking we've run

Y-you know what, maybe... that's the only choice, right? We've got to buy time somehow... Black Dawn, evasion, consequences... it sounds like a maze... do you think it's something from Mac's business dealings... and was this larger game that The Butcher mentioned connected to him, somehow?

Yeah, something to that, Elias, keep talking like that - you're buying us time. If it's Mac's business deal, I'd say the Black Dawn could be more than one story, like The Butcher said - maybe we're pawns, like you said, in a bigger game with more players... but the

(smirks) Ah, nice dance, Kael. You two are quite amusing - and so predictable. You think The Butcher will stop giving you breadcrumbs? Ha! This little maze is full of dead ends, and only one person will find their way to the exit... or will it just be a convenient hiding spot, leading me straight to The real game – but only because it's precisely on that exit that Black Dawn will change into the truth I intended.

The clock on the wall starts changing – counting down from one number to zero.

Suddenly, Vinnie Mac's face appears on a smaller screen to Elias' right. He looks uneasy.

Vinnie Mac: Look what I found, The Butcher... something important. Elias' phone. He received a text a day before we... (he glances around) before this.

A message appears on the larger wall – sent from Elias': Black Dawn - 8:45pm: -m Meet me at warehouse 5 to receive payment, come alone.

Elias' face contorts with confusion. Kaelie exchanges glances with him nervously. And the time on the countdown timer continues to drop.

The Butcher (leaning forward): And that's our next clue, boys and girls – a little bit about Elias' involvement before it all, a possible connection to a mysterious payment. Tell me: was your plan always to try and escape, or did you initially come in with someone else on

Oh... y-yes... I d-did come alone to meet who... I think it said Black Dawn, and I remember now, I had my phone on me, and I was in my car. What was I expecting, Kael? You... or... did I not think I would run into you that night?

Wait, Elias... I think your confusion is just part of his game... let's stall for time. You're right, though - I was your meeting spot, or so I hoped you wouldn't realize until now. You were expecting... me. We came close once, and I want you to think we did tonight too,

W-wait, no... no, I'm m-my wife's face is all I can... think about at that moment... I thought I'd found... I thought... we almost met... Kael, I remember now... we were going to meet, and it felt... it felt like we could've found something, or someone

(pausing, empathizing, and trying not to give anything away) Elias, stop. Please. Just play long enough. You're close to finding whatever you're looking for, in this maze, or maybe even the real reason why Snowflake's on our minds now...
Okay, Kaelie, calm down, they'll take us out soon... please, listen to me - we need to work together.
Calm down?! Are you insane?! I'm a dental assistant, not a bargaining chip for your amusement. We're at the mercy of some sadist, and you think working together will get us out?
No, no, listen, we'll be next. They want us to choose ourselves - if we don't, they kill us both! Don't you see what they're doing?!
Oh, great, now you're saying I'm a pawn just like everyone else?! This is sick, I won't go without a fight - and who's to say they won't kill the person we choose after I die, just for kicks!

That's a good discussion you two started, but it's taking its sweet time. Let me bring you a show to expedite things. Kaelie, I understand your concern for self-preservation — Elias is not as much a pawn as Kael the hyena. It seems Elias will have some more opportunities at freedom.

(The eerie laugh from Kaelie resounds)

And to show my fairness, I let Elias have a glimpse at how the person you've decided may live for 20 minutes before they're 'kicked out.'

No! No! Stop this! You can't do that to another person... to an innocent, I thought we had time to... please, don't let me hear their scream... (he turns around desperately) What am I supposed to do now, watch my fate being decided for another poor human?!

YOU DIDN'T EVEN BOTHER HIDING IT FROM ME, YOU INHUMANE MONSTER... I'm watching right along with you, Elias, this is some twisted freakshow, and I know I've got 40 seconds of 'mercy', if we count our time - you can die with me or watch an
NO! Don't let them choose, Kaelie, please! Let's take our chances, don't let them toy with us, I don't care if it's 1 in a million, it's better than giving in! Come on! We have to choose each other!
SHUT UP WITH THAT 1 IN A MILLION NONSENSE, ELEIAS, WE'RE ALREADY DOOMED, YOU'RE TRYING TO TRICK ME INTO SAVING YOU!

Oh, you people think you have a say in your own fate; that's cute. Your desperation is what makes my day, though. I'll give you this – Elias' desperation won over. And because of this... I'm extending you both five minutes; now, let me put a smile back on your faces. Since Kaelie's been quite vocal, perhaps a change in dynamics will suit both of your interests. You have five minutes. Make it worth my while.

(The speakers softly play a mix of melancholic and unsettling music.)

For the record, Kaelie's still got 25.9 seconds before you two will know whether she'll save herself and doom an innocent (Elias' friend – Alex, 26, an architect), or sacrifice herself for her new companion, which happens to be you, Mr. Elias, along with the same fate awaiting you both as a result.

Elias has just gained a tiny window – now he can make up for being the favourite that has been displayed in his little display. How will Elias react?
Kaelie, listen to me - we just bought a few more minutes, please think of Alex, don't let him... please have a heart, Kaelie, we can find another way, anything but this...

STOP IT, ELIAS, MY 'HEART' IS STILL RECOILED FROM THOSE CREEPY TAILES PLAYING RIGHT NOW, AND YOU THINK I CARE ABOUT SOME OTHER GUY'S FATE MORE THAN I DO MY OWN?! YOU'RE NOT THE FAVORED ONE NOW, I'M THE ONE IN CHARGE
Oh God, no, don't do this, Kaelie, please don't let me die - you'll know soon what they meant by 'kicking you out' - I'll be gone soon and it will be all in vain - Alex's fate...
YOU DARE USE MY CONCERNS AGAINST ME, ELEIAS?! WELL, HERE'S YOUR LUCK - A SECOND SPARED, AND IT'S MINE, NOT SOME FACELESS VICTIM LIKE ALEX...

Well, this is... refreshing. Kaelie's decided she's going to play both sides, or none at all, and try something else. (chuckles) Let me clarify some things – I'm still watching your little play unfold, Kaelie. The fate of these innocent lives depends on this dance of cat and mouse.

Since you think it wise, let's play it like that. As of now, the person you'll save will not be Alex; instead, it'll be the 28-year-old architect with Kaelin who happened to overhear her screams - or screams about someone getting a bad coffee. The 28-year-old architect with Kaelin, is also your last hope – and your fate.

However, you still haven't saved Elias; he's about two minutes away - unless...

I think it may be fun taking the choice for yourself out of the picture now that you're not even sure anymore; instead, I've devised a plan that involves letting you pick, but I'm not choosing the winner for either side just yet.

Here's how it's going:

A) Kaelie dies after a 3-minute period, saving 28-year-old architect with Kaelin, and sealing Elias' end 
B) A new participant with a choice in his or herself – Alex, enters in a 3-minute timer which allows him a 50.5% shot to save them selves, or
C) No
NO, PLEASE NO, Kaelie, don't make things more complicated, just decide, either way, one of us will still die... what difference does Alex make now?
SHUT UP FOR ONE SECOND AND LOOK AT THE TIMERS, ELEIAS - YOU'VE GOT UNDER 2 MINUTES LEFT - I DIDN'T EVEN NEED TO SAVE YOURSELF NOW, JUST MY OWN LIFE'S TIMERS JUST TURNED AGAINST ME...
Kaelie, look out the door, there's 28 floors to fall to... we'll know soon if it matters, if I even get out of here, which I won't because if I don't choose to die soon you will...
SHUT UP, YOU'RE GOING TO DRIVE ME FURTHER UP WALLS, I KNOW THE HEIGHT, I'VE SEEN IT FROM INSIDE - LOOK OUT THE 'WALL' AND TRY THINK OF A PLAN ALONG WITH YOUR LACK-OF-SUCCESS SKILLSET.

emotionalAuthenticity.notes: Elias' stuttering and hyperventilating are good, but Kaelie's anger and fear feel somewhat scripted at times. The Butcher's menace is consistent but sometimes feels a bit one-dimensional.

scenarioCoherence.notes: The physical space and mechanic are well-described, but there are some moments where the scenario feels a bit disconnected from the characters' experiences. The Butcher's control over the scenario is clear, but it's not always clear what the consequences of their actions are.

organicResolution.notes: The resolution feels somewhat earned, but it's not entirely clear what the characters' choices have led to. The Butcher's output of RESOLVED is clear, but it's not entirely clear what the implications of the agreement are.

turn.content: O-okay, just... calm down, alright?!  We need to, uh, figure this out, together! Kaelie, hi, I'm...  Elias, I teach history, I just woke up in... this...  What happened?

So you're the 'history expert', huh?  That's cute. Save it. You have 8 minutes to come up with some excuse for why you get spared. And don't even think about trying to make it me who dies! I'll never agree to that.
H-hey, please, don't talk to me like that! I'm not brave like some people might seem... . We need to calm down and find a way out...   If I must choose, I choose not to... be it, I mean. I need to go

Don't think you're that cute getting sappy now. You don't get to die without a reason, and it isn't pity. If there's actually something we can learn from this, maybe your history expertise will be useful... don't think you can just get off that easy. 7 minutes left.

You two are like a couple of annoying songbirds screaming at each other. Finally, something happens: you're both starting to see that I'm the least of your concerns. You're still fighting among yourselves more than you hate me. Five minutes left, and still, no agreement.

I don't care what kind of 'excuse' Kaelie needs, or whatever 'history expertise' Elias thinks he has. What I need is one of you deciding to go. Don't say a word. Just nod.

And I better see one nod from one of you in the next minute or there won't be anyone left to 'agree' after all.
Oh... oh no... okay, I'll... I'll nod. Just please, don't make me do anything...

So now it's you who are the weak one. Thinking you're above this but nodding because you're too scared. Well, it worked out pretty easy for you. 4 minutes left. Now you get to decide whose life it will be. What's it going to be, history boy?
No, please, it can't come down to that! I was trying to avoid, uh, choosing someone, I guess. 3 minutes left...

Save the 'gutted victim' act for the Butcher, you're just making it easier for yourselves to choose, and I'm the obvious pick. I'm more awake, I'm the threat, and I can handle a fight if that makes it worth dying here.

Enough drama, children. It's not a popularity contest. Either decide between you, or I decide for you. And trust me, you won't want that.

Time's running out, and I'm getting impatient. I don't see Kaelie agreeing anytime soon, so... one and a half minutes left... I think you've made it too easy for me, Elias.

But, fine. It seems you're not willing to make the choice either. Well, Kaelie, it seems like the history boy has decided for you. You're next.
N-n-no, wait please don't do this...  I... I was going to, I didn't want Kaelie to die, but the clock was going to run out too... I didn't choose, she was just going to die anyway...

Don't make excuses now, Elias. You were the spineless weaklink. One minute left. The clock isn't even giving us any more time to pretend it doesn't have anything to do with it. You're going to sit here, still whining, while I die for being the strong, hard choice? No thanks

K-k-kelie wait, stop, I'm going to do it, I was going to try to convince her but if I have to choose it'll be the coward's death! Kaelie, no, I swear, it has nothing to do with it being you, the other person, it was just that I...

Shut up, Elias! Don't apologize or rationalize it now. If I must listen to your pathetic choice, just get on with it. And don't even think for a second this isn't because of me being the strongest choice... The butcher's hand comes up with a meat hook, it looks at the clock:

Enough, enough of the pitiful excuses. Time's almost up. And as much as it hurts you to see it now... Kaelie, you've won yourself the privilege of being second, not just for your determination, but because Elias made his decision, finally.

The clock ticks down... 10 seconds.

Kaelie, with a look of bitter defiance, looks at Elias then finally decides, and finally, at me. I take up more air and a louder voice. I raise my massive, blacksmithing hammer. Kaelie, realizing all the while, her time has arrived: with my final breath of a growl and I strike...

CRUSH...

Kaelie's head shatters, her scalp torn from her skull by the mighty hammer swing. The sound is like a hollow steel pipe. Kaelie's eyes, now bloodshot, empty, are fixed in horror, frozen at the second of impact. Her life force gushes away. 

Blood explodes across the walls, and from Kaelie's shredded neck flows down her body like the rain outside. Her eyes stare lifeless and frozen while I lift the hammer with its mangled, splatter-coated business end back up into position at Elias.
NOOOO!!!!! KAYYYYYEEEE! MY WIFE, MY WIFE WILL WORRY, WHAT IF IT'S NOT OVER FOR ME?! PLEASE, GOD, MERCY! YOU CAN'T, YOU JUST TOLD ME, WE WERE SUPPOSED TO DECIDE... NOT... YOU!  hyperventil

Kaelie's jaw, still locked in defiance from a moment ago, freezes in permanent scream, her lips stretched in a macabre grimace of terror as her vision begins to fade to black. In her thoughts: I WON, I WON, I WAS RIGHT I AM STRONG, STRONG LIKE THE HAMMER, CRUSHING
IT'S KAYYYYYEEEE... IT'S MY FAULT...
Kaelie's voice becomes a death-filled murmur: F... fool... was supposed to be my time... not yours... look at me... see my face... it's all that matters.
I'm shaking, my face is slick with sweat... Why is she talking like that? No, no one dies, no one dies, Kaelie!
Don't you dare try to reason with me! I'm not listening to your spineless crap, get a grip – we need a plan, a real one, or we're dead – not this weepy, 'I don't wanna die' nonsense!
Okay, okay, plan... just give me something... anything! We have to escape, don't we? The doors must open if... if it's not just stuck...

You think we're escaping out there because the doors magically work?! This isn't a stuck elevator, Elias, this is sabotage – something went terribly wrong and now we're playing Russian roulette with our lives – and I'm not willing to bet on those odds!

Ah, excellent teamwork, Elias and Kaelie. Excellent. Both scared out of their minds and already blaming each other. That's just fine by me. Now, as the minutes tick away, I'll keep you company with my favorite hobby: hearing a person beg. Go on, Elias, I'm fascinated, tell Kaelie about it.

(suddenly raises voice)
Tell her, Eli! Say it all out loud: We will never escape. We will never leave this place alive. (pauses) And that door won't magically open, you know why? Because I've personally seen to that. You two are here to die. And that clock keeps ticking...
No, it can't be... I don't believe you, that's... my life, Kaelie, my family, my whole... why are you doing this to us?! This is just wrong; we're just average people!

Shut up, Elias, just shut your damned mouth for one second - don't beg, don't plead, don't even breathe like that – I hear you, the Butcher knows it won't work, so let's make it easy, just stop talking!
You're calm, cold, Kaelie... don't make me talk, don't make us be in this anymore, think something – anything... please, help...

For once in your precious life, get some fight in you, Elias – it's me who needs to think, it's me who needs a plan, and it's going to be something you're not prepared to like, get used to it – and by the way, my name is not going to help you here, so

(raises an eyebrow as a cold, sadistic grin spreads across my face)
Oh, how lovely, how quaint, Kaelie - defending her... (not her, her name's not on your lips) and now suddenly concerned with strategy. This is delightful. Your inner monologue is like a show I'm not paying for - entertaining nonetheless. But to get back on topic, Eli, you see, begging doesn't help. And neither do excuses - your life and that life (jests pointing towards Kaelie) hanging by a fraying thread, with only seconds to choose who it's going to be. (raises the hammer, examining it for a moment)

(suddenly starts chuckling softly)
You know, I almost want you two to come up with something brilliant, because this might be the most... thrilling... this time. (wiping a bead of sweat from forehead with forearm, a cold gleam dancing behind your eyes)
This can't be... someone's enjoying this... it's madness...

No kidding?! This isn't breaking news, genius. Of course, they're enjoying this - I'm dying up in here, and they're getting a kick out of it. Just peachy – great, great, just more of why I hate humanity right now...
Kaelie? stop, just talk, try to make sense of this - maybe there's another way... maybe you think of something... anyone would try, you see... please... just keep talking...

Don't make me sick with your weak, whingy words, Elias – talk?! I've been trying to think of ways to break out of here, and what do I get in return? Please and try? You think I need encouragement to be clever? I need a chance, that's what I need

(takes a step forward, and the floor creaks under my weight) Oh, Eli. So devoted. You really care for her that much, don't you? (eyes flick across Kaelie's tense face) Even now - knowing you could both meet the same grisly fate. Adorbs. (leaning against my blacksmithing hammer, with hands draped lazily over it)

(in voice low but controlled and sinister)

But you're getting a little ahead of yourselves, lovebirds - I have more tricks up this black suit. For you two, no 'chance.' You've got me, and that hammer. And when I'm bored (raises an eyebrow, and my stare locks onto the pair) it usually means... (pausing long, then chuckling darkly) someone loses. Kaelie, my, oh how brave you're getting, but bravery dies hard, and so does everyone here soon. You two will see each person of those words. And then? Well - I might even take Elias's words to heart when I carve that last sentence into his dying mind. (flicks an iron-tipped hand around to the sound, making both jump, their faces twisting together in desperation as you grin once more.)
Stop, just... just think... about anything... not a plan, just... anything to make this less sickening...

You can stop the fake charade with the cute nicknames - lovebirds? Are you seriously trying to humanize this monster act? Because, seriously? You're killing me - literally - inside this elevator - no plan, okay? But I will not be 'brave.' Nor, I won't be dying.
Kaelie, don't be ridiculous, we can't – just stop, there has to be something, someone, who's not a participant...

Oh, don't be obtuse, Elias – I've been trying to remember every single soul I know, the dentists, the receptionists, the people in the break room... and there is someone - my best friend from dental school, Samantha, works downtown in an adjacent office building - no way I can reach her,
Wait... what... who's this Butcher? Why would he want... us to kill each other? My head's spinning, I need... time to think.
My mind's a complete... emptiness. I don't even remember how I got here, or who I am. Wait, think Kaelie, think, what's my art like, what do I create... a spark, a flame... it's all I have left.
Art... Kaelie's an artist... That reminds me, my son liked to paint... colors all over the house, like he had a world that was just his to play in... my Emily... my happy Emily...

Stop it, Elias, don't say their names, don't remind myself. I don't know anyone. Emily, a painter, a child... it's all coming back now, snippets. Colors bleeding off the canvas, my world's like that, fleeting...

(amused, a slow smile spreading over their face) Oh, Kaelie and Elias, already dancing around each other's past... How... precious. You two cling so tightly to your loved ones, your identities. It's lovely to see. But what's really important is that time is short. Tick-tock, ticking away. You need to focus on the real question, not your nostalgia. Which one of you is disposable? Is it worth fighting, worth sacrificing to keep the survivor free?

(barely above a whisper) No, we don't have to do this... my life has already been taken from me, it's been a year and a half... I've been searching, wondering why... But it shouldn't come down to me killing you, Kaelie or... the guilt, what more do I want

(her voice laced with defiance) You don't know me, or my purpose. If my art, my spark dies, I'm nothing. But I refuse to choose between us, to kill the one possibility of freedom left... We have to find a way out of this, together, or not at all.

(slowly, resignation settling over him) Together, then. We find a way... for the sake of a life that once was... for Emily... (pauses, looking away) I know who I'll choose if you force me to now... but it won't make any sense to myself, only in trying to make up

Don't... don't do it, Elias. Think of a solution, any way out. We have... (straining her memories) a pattern on my wrist... my art was a grid, a canvas waiting to be filled. There might be more, hidden details in my past. Look, really look, for something that

(satisfied with the development, their smile growing wider) Ah, the perfect ingredients for a delicious game. Two fragile, desperate, and conflicted souls. You're both thinking so creatively, Elias and Kaelie – though I wouldn't have it any other way. This isn't going to be a choice that comes easily; every second that passes weighs heavier on your consciences. You're so caught up in survival, you nearly forgot I'm watching you – studying you. Your patterns are already fascinatingly clear, even to those who only glimpse the surface.

(Pacing near the chains, inspecting every link and fixture)

Now, let's discuss your pattern idea, Kaelie. You say your art was a canvas. You may indeed hold a hidden message within yourself, waiting to burst forth. (Leaning closer, an unsettling closeness) It would make sense if I told you there's more to this place than what you see. Every inch has been calculated – every sound, everything has a purpose... There might just be enough information to break free, hidden within yourself and around you. But for now, your thoughts are still muddle, a mixture of emotions – a true symphony of desperation.

(Eyes shifting between Elias and Kaelie, anticipating the game unfolding)

(vacantly staring at Kaelie) A pattern, a life to find... and you, in it, somehow... the threads of mine and hers, tangled, yet apart, just like... my Emily, my happy life, and now this emptiness... I'm trying to remember...

(her gaze locking onto Elias, a mixture of desperation and determination) I see something in you, Elias. A fragmented memory that echoes me. My paintings often explored duality, the connection between creation and destruction. There's a way for us to tap into it, to reveal a hidden path... I need you to find the

(eyes narrowing, the faint memory of his son's nursery slowly piecing together) a music box, a lullaby, 'In Dreams We Dwell'... Emily's favorite, but... there's something else tied to it, the song reminds me of darkness... of loss... (struggling for words, as if trapped

Kaelie's eyes flash with recognition. No... the nursery... Her gaze darts towards Elias's shackled wrist. Look at... look at my watch. Her gaze snaps back to Elias. Does my art remind you of anything? In her desperation, a connection seems almost within reach.

(stunned for a moment, taken aback by Kaelie's rapid recall) Ah, music box lullabies, loss, darkness – you're touching on something important, Elias. The fragments are beginning to surface. I must admit, I'm entertained. (Their grin has grown so sharp it's starting to take on a jagged contour)

(Remerging from their brief silence)

Look closer, Kaelie. There, on Elias's wrist, it matches. The watch we gave her, its engraving now resonating within your mind - it's connected to something, a pattern that you thought was mere chaos, but isn't. It's more than that – a bridge of memories, of experiences. The way you two intertwine your fragments is creating something entirely unique – a new reality you'd both love, yet be horrified by at the same time. 

(A swift and sudden hand motion, the sound of a mechanical switch engaging; an ear-piercing whistle pierces the room, the fluorescent lights above flickering erratically)

We're almost done with just observations; it's now our turn to manipulate reality. This moment you both feel hope... hope that is short-lived. The mechanics are taking over, forcing you down paths neither of you truly chose. The choice is not in front of you but is inside both of you.

(The Butcher disappears behind a veil of distorted mirrors)

Time's up; it becomes a game of who survives

(flinching, overwhelmed, as memories flood back) Emily's watch, it matches hers... her engraving - an eagle soaring into darkness... the day it came into the world... the weight of it now presses down... Kaelie, she's connected, too... a web of threads starts to unravel... we must hold

(her voice rises above the chaos, eyes blazing with a determined urgency) I see it, Elias. I see it... the eagle soaring. Emily's story, intertwined with yours, now intersects with my creation. I see the web, the threads that weave everything together. The watch... its design... (tensing against the

(gritted teeth, struggling to speak past the mechanical hiss) the eagle's shadow, it falls on... on me, the weight, but there's another, a shadow of light... (eyes locked on Kaelie) your canvas, it was more than just art, more than just survival... it was a bridge,

(tensing, her voice urgent) That was... the other side! Not just darkness, but... not just life. You remember it? A place, or a time? (frantically she tries to grasp onto Elias's hand with hers that are strapped by the cables) Elias, look for it! Find it! The

turn.content: P-please... p-peace, let us talk... W-wait, which one of us d-dies?! No, no, no! You can't do this to us! Oh no, this can't be real, not after she's g-gone!
What do you mean after she's gone? What are you talking about? And why am I even stuck here?! We need to focus on getting out of this hellhole, not... whoever you're worrying about!

M-my wife... my wife passed away six months ago... I... I just thought I couldn't lose anyone else... You're r-right, s-sorry... I got sidetracked... Oh p-please, let's try, we have to work t-together.
Wait, okay, focus. If you and I decide which one of us dies, how do you know The Butcher will actually follow through on it? Can we trust him at all?

That's a wise question, Kaelie. Let me answer it for you. I'd tell you to trust me, if only to keep things... interesting. However –  I'm only here to provide options and entertainment, not a promise. Your safety, in this entire endeavor, isn't exactly... guaranteed. What Elias or yourself will be doing between now and then is entirely up to yourselves. And as for you, Elias, your loss a month ago... I believe it was... not six months. Don't be too distracted by it and make me have to take a moment to think about your past mistakes. 

Seven and a half...
I-I think he's right... we have to rely on ourselves...  I made a mistake, my wife passed away six months... not after something recent.  Sorry, it's just really hard...
Don't apologize, just get a grip. This isn't some kind of twisted therapy session – it's about surviving whatever is going to happen next. I need to ask,

Y-what did you want... ask?! Oh, right, you need to know something about your s-life, before the cargo ship docks...  D-do we get to decide the details of, u-uh, the chosen person's, d-death?! How exactly...?!

Ugh, that's sick. You're asking me to care about whether or not I get to choose how I die? But if I have to... Can we kill me quickly and painlessly, or will it be some twisted game, watching me suffer?

Oh, Kaelie, your skepticism is so... refreshing. You'd rather live a little bit longer in this miserable place, wouldn't you? As for the terms of your... demise, let's make it simple. The chosen person decides. Yes, they get to pick the nature of your... event, within certain... boundaries. Think of me more as a director and less as a surgeon. And if they decide to make you suffer, I suppose... that's on them.

And you're getting warmer with these minutes left, folks. Four minutes to make a choice, or to start wondering which buttons to press on the countdown. You may have some guesses that'll bring the time back up or speed things up further, but don't try that just yet.

Tell me Elias – it's time to weigh the pros and cons. Does Kaelie trust you not to kill your own self, to help keep her alive? Can I trust the two of you to actually decide anything? Clock keeps ticking... (smirking)

N-no, I... think about... trust me? I d-don't know if I deserve that... I... made promises to Sarah, and I let her down already, b-but I want to try, for... for both of us... Maybe... please, trust me, for now. Can you t-trust me...

(scoffs) Elias, I don't know what's more ridiculous – your attempts to sound sincere or your decision to make promises to a dead woman. Can I trust you to survive this if you make that decision for yourself too?

I...  I'm not trying to be r-ridiculous... or make foolish p-promises... K-kicking myself would be pointless now... S-surviving isn't even the question, is it? We either get out alive, or... one of us does, alone. Y-yes...

Alright, Elias, enough with the self-doubt. If I make you stay in here and I die quickly, do you get Snowflake, my rabbit...? You know it's all I care about – I'll never let you leave her behind if I had any say in my own end. Two minutes left

(laughs dryly) And yet, Elias, here you are trying to be convincing again. 'Pointless now,' indeed. It's touching.

And Kaelie, you'd make promises for that little pet, Snowflake, wouldn't you? That says everything. As for you, two minutes left, indeed.

Snowflake, the white rabbit. That's a name that might be... interesting. Considering what you two think of your circumstances, the story behind a rabbit might change everything. I, instead, choose to keep Snowflake in the narrative for the moment, for both of us. What do you, Kaelie, want to be true about Snowflake to make the choice to stay in the room – a desperate attempt or something more meaningful?

And don't waste any more words, people; time is running short.

If you decide I should take S-Snowflake and s_survive, p promise me you'll l-like the truth about her... the story I know... it's b-beautiful, even here in this h-hideous place... maybe it'll help keep us together...

If I stay, Elias, I want to believe that you and Snowflake will make it to a place where I can be laid to rest near some sunshine – the real thing. I want to think it'll be better than being stuck in some dark, damp hole on my own. Don't promise me that, though - just
P-promised. K-keep... s-say good things about me to Sarah, for me.
(firmly) I'll say she loved you, truly loved you, even at the darkest of times. Now I need you to do something for me – get outside, get the help, don't think of me as you do this... go...
I stutter...  Y-yes, s-sorry. I d-don't know what t-this is about... p-please don't t-kill us if we c-can just... talk about this.
Okay, stay calm and think for real, okay Snowflake's counting on us... Elias, can you move? What're they going to say if you just, like, casually try to get free?

E-E-Elias... N-N-no! If I try, t-they'll... they'll catch us. T-ten minutes isn't e-enough time for me to... move anything anyway, s-she's too tight. This gr-grate won't give... strains against the chains, wincing with the pain as
Don't move, okay? It's okay. Alright, we have to think, and fast. If I get free and try and untie you, what if I fail?

How... thrilling this conversation is. You two think so much, so desperately. Allow me to interject. Kaelie's attempt to free her will fail, because that's the nature of the chains I've used. You can try, Snowflake. Just like you can think, and scream, until the clock says otherwise. I made sure that the only thing holding us here are the chains - there's a slight... chance, for either to get free. It's up to the both of you.

S-S-stop c-calling her names! S-she has nothing to do w-with... why can't we just work together, p-please?!  If the chains have a s-slight chance, I-I suppose... it's possible... if I can t-tug my foot
Whoa, Elias, easy, don't try anything till you're ready. See when I give you a sign, okay? Maybe our odds improve if we both time it right. Are you thinking the same?
Y-yes... same... time it r-right... s-smart plan maybe?  Wh-what's the sign?
My left shoe's been sliding down this whole time... I'll tap my heel 5 times when I'm set. Don't move until I do. Five taps. Ready?

Ah, teamwork. What a beautiful... concept. The anticipation is growing, and soon, one of you will have to put their friend to rest. I'll enjoy watching this little exchange of faith... It seems like you are about to try your little escape. Very predictable.  Don't get impatient if the tap doesn't happen right away; we've still got six whole minutes left in the game...
O-okay... I-I hear... left shoe... be s-settled... and... five taps. I-I'll c-count on it... f-fingers crossed... w-wait.
One tap now.
F-f-five m-minutes left... c-count it right... .
5 tap - .

(chuckles) Oh, how sweet. You've counted it down with such... desperation. It's almost cute. Elias, do you feel your foot move a little, ever so slightly, when she tapped that last beat? Maybe your ankle is... wobbling ever so subtly. Tell me, Elias, what do you plan to move, exactly?
N-no... that's... that's the only s-sign Kaelie's got? I-I thought... five tap's... my g-go signal... I'll s-tug anyway! I'll t-tug my...
Ah, yeah, now, Elias! Come on! Don't make me wait, go for it!
G-got that... g-going as fast as I... try to p-pull free...
Come on, Elias, I'm trying too, the metal is scraping against my skin!
(Elia's voice barely above a whisper) Easy, girl... calm down. Don't trust anything he says. Can't be too careful. Look at you, those chains will rip your clothes.

Shh, don't waste your breath trying to calm me down... I'm a diver, I know how to assess a situation! I've taken damage before!  We HAVE to break these chains or find a way out, now. We can decide who lives later. Let's work together and...

(interrupting) Save it, marine. You don't know me. I've seen people go down on bad decisions, and it doesn't end pretty. I'd rather make someone else decide who I get to breathe for another six minutes than trust fate. The clock ain't on our side.

(firmly, but slightly calmer) Alright, okay... let's talk this out. Whoever is going to go has to be agreed upon. We only have one minute before that Butcher guy intervenes, which means we need to think strategically, think who we lose the least by sending that person to die...

(skeptical, unblinking gaze) The clock's at sixty seconds. And the both of you can talk, analyze, and speculate for that time. But I'll offer you a little help: your choices won't change your inevitable fate. Whoever lives, they'll be... (he pauses, studying their reactions) ...interesting. The choice, for your purposes, might seem like the difference between life and death, but for one of you, it's going to be the difference between nothing and a... delayed nothing. So tell me, what makes you think either of you can out-manipulate me, or the game set before you?
(looking away from Kaelie, focusing on the clock and the Butcher) Games... you think this is some twisted game to you? It should tell you something that you think it's a game.

I think Elias is right; it's not a game, it's... it's like he's watching some terrible, awful show, where it doesn't matter whose name is at the bottom of the bill. We need to focus on how to escape; I've spotted some ventilation system nearby, but we'll never reach it.
(eyes lingering on Kaelie) Ventilation system? Where is it? That's where my buddy went... if I'd known better, he'd be alive now. You think it's that simple, getting out of here? This thing's a tomb.

No, it's not simple, I'm not naive... I've seen my share of sunken ships too. But we can use our skills to try. My background is diving, I know how water breaches work... that ventilation system might be our only hope for getting air while he's...

(leaning back in his chair, an almost amused smile spread across his face) You two are like two insects trapped in a spider's web, squirming and trying to bite one another in desperation. You're close, though... your instincts are correct. It is a tomb... a tomb from which I've salvaged the remains, rearrangement of pieces, and crafted a... game... you've stumbled into. Ventilation system, interestingly, won't be necessary. It won't be long before my timekeeper marks its completion. You're running out, faster than you might know. Kaelie's experience as a diver should be telling her something about air supply...

(growing cold, eyes narrowing) You want something, you've got an angle. What is it with air supply? Don't play dumb with us, what are you hiding, and how's this tied to our chances of making it out alive?

Air supply, it's... the oxygen levels are dropping fast, the generators are malfunctioning...  our air mask's supposed to last us a short while longer because the submarine's air scrubbers kicked in before, but with the hull damaged... the scrubbers might have shut down or failed, making every second count for us not

(grim resolve in his voice) Alright, then. It's a straight shot now. We're not getting out of here, but if I can stall you that little bit longer, Kaelie might - I can try breaking free, take the hit for us both if you're the one who needs it. (he pulls at

No, Elias! Don't be crazy! I can handle it... or rather, you know, try. Your marine biology skills might just give... us a way out. The jellyfish thing, the obsession... could that be tied to something?

(eyes never leaving Elias as he tries to break free) Ah, Elias's plan, so brave or simply selfless... we shall see. Marine biology and, of course, jellyfish. Interesting combination, especially considering his... (eyes flicker towards a screen to the side with the word Jellyfish emblazoned on it in bold, red letters) ...enthusiasm. His knowledge might indeed be key, and the game's... stakes are high enough to be worth exploring this possibility. It also tells me you're close to figuring it out; you almost grasp how everything's linked – my interests, my creations, your fates.

(He leans forward, and his smile sharpens.)

However, as much as I'm intrigued by your potential, I will need you to make the choices now, for your entertainment. Your 'obsession' as you might say has caught my attention, Kaelie. You, on the other hand, are a calculated risk and could possibly turn your own life... or another's into a 'delayed nothing.' Now, your options are... I propose this: I can keep to one or you choose to give me the name – I'll let that person take one step back for the moment – and for the next minute, you're free... well, relatively, considering where we are.

(grunting in frustration, failing to break free) Give him something, Kaelie... let him have that one second's peace. You, of all people, know our chances are already against us. Maybe your 'dive plan' can help when it matters, and that's not in the next minute. (he looks
Fine. I'll... name Elias, to take a step back. Please, keep that screen showing. If he has to, he can use his experience to get us as far as he can.

(exhaling heavily, accepting the risk) You should've let me stall, Kaelie... now, he'll just use this chance to set the trap for us. That screen shows our fate: a Jelliflux project of his, a twisted thing with your obsession at its core... I see it now

No, no, Elias! He's just manipulating you. You need to remember what you know, the project's risks, its connections... don't let him get in your head. The jellyfish, Jelliflux must be a bio-lab test gone wrong.

turn.content: Stuttering, I say, 'W-we... don't wanna die. Let's t-talk about this. H-how's p-perfume smell like lavender involved...'
Enough out of you, don't try to spin this to your advantage. I've got a better chance of escaping alive than you. My focus was on one goal, you just stumbled in here like a damsel.
Oh no... n-no, that's m-my w-wife you're t-talking to... the lavender. Y-you don't u-understand, it's all that I ha-have of her now... please don't h-hate me, let me s-try...

Cut the act, I'm not buying it. Save your fake emotions, I can see the real desperation in those eyes, and I'm going to use it. We may not like each other, but I need one of us in a clear state of mind, and that isn't happening with you spewing out grief. Make

(Chuckles, clearly intrigued by Kaelie's sharp instincts) Ah, how quickly you pick up on the situation, don't you? You two are perfect opposites - and, yet, you may be perfect foils for my games. Six minutes, 30 seconds. As I watch, you make your move. Kaelie, if you think you have what it takes to survive, go ahead and make a suggestion to Elias. Perhaps your cold logic can be the thing that saves you both? But be warned, I watch your every move, and Elias, if you surrender to his emotions once more, the consequences won't be in your favor any further...
Y-you see... Kaelie... see... I w.want out of here t-to live for... m-me and my children... my w-wife's s-souls, I mean... they need me...

I hear what you're saying, Elias, I actually think I can relate. We both need a way out, me for Snowflake, you... whatever is driving you. Look, I'm willing to make a deal, but you need to be rational in return if we want to stay alive. If I propose a plan to
O-okay... pl-plan... p-please...  I-I'll l-try to l-listen...

Alright, listen carefully then, because you're not going to like this part, but we need to focus. Here's what we do: I make sure Snowflake and my family are rescued, and in return - or so my plan tells me - you help me figure out who the Butcher is, and we use that information

(Grins, intrigued by Kaelie's suggestion) Oh, you two are more interesting than I thought. You see, Elias and Kaelie are considering an offer of rescue, one tied to cooperation - not survival. Five minutes, and already, our game has taken an unexpected turn. But I suppose that's why I play games with my guests - to observe the dynamics, to study human nature.

(Steps forward, eyes gleaming with a mixture of interest and cruelty)

The Butcher continues: Kaelie, I must admit, your idea isn't bad... if a bit naive. The trade-off: Snowflake for the identity of the Butcher. It's an intriguing proposition, one I'm willing to consider. Elias, as your part of the deal, if you want your children to be rescued, you'll have to provide me the names of at least two of them first. Once that happens, I might just grant Kaelie's plan. However, Elias, be warned, the fate for one, at the very least, might still end up here with us tonight. Your decision: reveal their names, or keep them safe.
Oh no... n-no children... can't d-decide... what if I r-reveal the wrong... w-wrong one...

That's not an option, I don't need someone whose kids might be expendable anyway. But since the Butcher thinks this is an either-or situation, Elias, it's time to be rational: we make my getting out of here dependent on the identity of the Butcher. Tell me something, something true, that you
O-okay... K-a-ael, t-trust me on s-something? M-my first k-kid's nam-mme... Emily... was g-going to p-p-paint the r-rooouu in pink... when s-she g-ggrew up. (Whispers) No one kn-knows

That means nothing - a trivial secret that doesn't put your kids in this mess directly. Fine, it's out then, Elias, Emily's going to paint the roof pink. With this, I can try to stall the clock, let's make a play. To the Butcher, I say: now we've got something

(Sits back, amused by Kaelie's quick thinking) Ah, how predictable you two are. Kaelie's trying to turn my own game around on her, using Elias's trivial secret as leverage. Three minutes, 45 seconds to go, and I'm still considering the dynamics of this game.

(Leans forward, eyes fixed on Kaelie)

The Butcher smiles: Kaelie, you should be applauded for trying. The fact that your attempt at subversion is weak doesn't diminish it. Yet, this conversation isn't just about games; it's also about information flow. So, with your admission that your 'play' won't actually help your cause... or Elias's, I decide to make this turn in another way. Elias, Emily, a child you're holding so close in mind, will not be the one who paints the roof pink – she won't be a reminder, if you continue to play this game, of love; more likely of regret, of choice and of the pain left behind... or should.

(Stares sharply at Elias)

Will that seal Emily's fate?

(Kaelie and Elias freeze, weighing the Butcher's ominous remark, the implications of what could or already has come next)

(T-stammering) Y-why would she be in... p-pain...? I-I just thought, when she... when she grows up, she wants to... paint... the r-roof a certain way... (Stammers, eyes fixed on the Butcher's face, fear rising)

Stop it right there. Enough games. You're not even sure if that threat would be relevant to Elias right now, because what he's just said, is what's true. This isn't about Emily or kids. Emily is a trivial secret, and that's what we should keep emphasizing, not give your twisted game any more

(Desperate, stammering) I-I t-told you, it's th-trivial... Emily w-want-to p-p-put pink paint on the r-roof... nothing more... K-a-ael said it w-works... (Frustrated) Stop m-m-making it m-meansingless

Elias, stop talking, think: whatever's been done, is irrelevant now. To the Butcher, it's clear what's next, either of us tries to give something meaningful, something that matters for your twisted purpose, so, let's get this over with already – tell me where Snowflake is or how to get out
Stuttering badly... 'W-wh-what's... th-this, p-plugin? T-too simple... How do I, h-how can I s-split my fate from... yours?
You think this plugin's simple? We're about to become pawns in some sick game, and you're worried about how I died? I look at the raccoon - might be our first clue. How's your thinking right now?
St-stuff. M-my mind's a s-stressful place... Th-thinking's not really working at m-moment. Wh-what's s-so wrong with the r-raccoon here, huh?  Wh-what if...

Ahh no, stop that, Elias. I know your thinking's foggy, but let's focus. If we don't figure this out, I'm more afraid of leaving that raccoon here alone - for some reason, it looks almost hopeful. What's going on there might save us if we use your... uh,

Ah, don't get all sappy over that raccoon, Kaelie, it's a spectator in this game, nothing more. Elias, your mind's in a whirl, but try to piece things together. Think of the raccoon as a... reminder. It could die too, if one of you chooses incorrectly. What I like about my game is the... unpredictable nature of it. No script, just players like two lost souls like yourselves, trying to stay on track long enough to live another day. And now it sounds like one of them - Kaelie here - senses an opening, or maybe the stink of my game makes some part of you want to escape the fate that's been handed to you? Whatever makes you feel something - let's go with that, because your emotions are the currency in this game.
I s-spared many creatures in h-hunting, b-but they d-divided my w-will to h-hunt, my reason to g-go on. Th-the raccoon's... hopefu... l-life here, it's like... I w-remember when

Wait, hold on, something connects Elias's past and that raccoon... You hunted to keep going, but the raccoon here might remind you of something that keeps you from wanting to hunt, from wanting to hurt. If it's hope, then we can cling to the fact that there's more than fate written for us.

H-hope? L-like that. The raccoon... it's small, innocent... my s-soul remembers... a child in a d-den of h-hypocrisy and... 'No. I can try again. What does my s-story hold that can give us leverage, a chance for both of us to... not...

Elias, I think I get it... Your life as a hunter was a choice that consumed you, but your memories of that little boy, or Snowflake – I feel like you mentioned something about a fluffy rabbit... You held on to something innocent, and maybe that's what can break The Butcher's game.

(leaning forward, intrigued) A child... A 'Snowflake'? Fluffy white rabbit? You're getting close, Elias. I think you might even remember what it is. You've held onto this hope, or whatever it is, for a long time - it's a shame I've probably killed more like that child, that rabbit. (leaning in even closer, a morbid interest in his tone) Tell me, what is it that kept you hunting? Was it just the skill, the challenge? Maybe, I thought... the memories? That's where it gets interesting if our two game players - you, Elias - actually believe in redemption. It seems like there is a part of your past with that raccoon and child's memories.

St-stammering. Memories... They came back, and... my w-wife, Sarah. She saw Snowflake too... that fluffy white... she wanted a family. We c-couldn't have children. S-she adopted the b-boy I hunted for, that w-white thing with fur so soft... Snowflake... The

(tears starting to well up in her eyes) No, don't. Elias, stop. That... the pain, the love, the guilt... if we keep this up, we're not just playing for our own lives; I'm thinking of Snowflake, too. Elias, we need to look at each other,
(voice cracking) W-wife... L-live through her... my love. Kaelie... look for a chance to break this, for both of us, to...

I see it, Elias. We need to find a connection, something in this despicable setup that speaks to the pain of losing loved ones... and maybe that's not just losing ourselves. (gazing directly at Elias) Snowflake, your wife, and... what about our own story?

(leaning back, eyes narrowing slightly, a hint of a smile creeping onto his face) Ah, Kaelie, always reaching for something deeper. You think you've grasped the thread, but let's weave it a bit more into this twisted fabric I've created. Elias, you have memories of your wife and child, but what about Snowflake, the fluffy white rabbit? It seems your memories have been clouded, hidden away from your conscious self, or maybe I should say... 'suppressed'. Your wife adopted the young boy, didn't she? The game is shifting, I see the dynamics starting to align. Now, what Kaelie just intimated is... what our players have been running from is... (leaning forward, speaking slowly, eyes fixed on each of them) 'the loss of something more than just themselves'. This setup is a maze of emotional pain, love, and loss - you're starting to understand, but there's a catch in there too, hidden in the darkness I created. How much can Elias remember? And, more precisely, what does that have to do with their chance - together - at survival...

No, Snowflake... can't remember everything. But a face, a smell... a little boy, holding a b-bunny cage at the market, a kind boy with eyes... so like mine. The Butcher, you m-may not be looking for what you think... my memories have just as much weight as the love

(eyes darting between the raccoon and Elias) Wait, if Elias remembers holding a bunny cage, there might be connections – not just love and loss – but something more tangible. I remember your Snowflake was a reminder, a comfort, a symbol of hope, and you, Eli's, was a way for his wife

My cage... I kept it with m-me. Always with m-me... the one I used to buy food for Snowflake at the m-market. The r-rabbit was just an illusion for me... I-I couldn't keep living... so I'd feed a different b-bird every time to give... my wife... something to

(gulps) No... no, stop, Elias. The cages aren't just for food, are they? He gave you one, this... 'Butcher.' You gave one with every memory of feeding Snowflake, a cage meant not for hunger but for memories; for a chance to live again.

(confused, gazing away) My brother... his words sound nice when they say that, but... this isn't the Ferris wheel he went on. This is something different. (pauses, looking at Kaelie) Is it time... for you to be the sacrifice, Kaelie? Can you... tell

No way, freakin' get your priorities straight, dude - we need a distraction to cut these wires and get out of here, we can't start killing each other yet! Who cares about the countdown, it's just a publicity stunt, right? Think about it, a social media sensation, us being stuck on a doomed

(lost in thought, gazing at the countdown timer) No stunt, Kaelie. That's not a stunt. It's a reminder. A clock to match my... other losses. My brother didn't have a choice either. (stares at her with a hint of desperation) But how... how do we trust anyone

Oh come on, trust isn't an option, we do what it takes - and right now, that means I'd rather trust my survival instincts, not this creepy guy and sure as hell, not his countdown, so let's focus on getting us outta here before that thing goes live or The Butcher actually sticks to his

You want out. Okay, let's play with the math. Assume I give you a 30-second window, and you don't fail, you cut those wires, you both walk, and this isn't real. Sounds about right. But you know what makes it harder? You've been stuck here longer than you think. That 30 seconds would've passed when I told you about me being 'The Butcher.'

(tense, trying to grasp time and reality) 30 seconds... more than I think? (looks frantically around for any sign of passage) But what was the first time you saw the ride? Was it the operator's logbook you showed, and... and when? What year... I want the year...

Who's keeping track of years here?! Shut up, it's irrelevant - just answer, Elias, is that time-losing trick of his even close to relevant in this 'time-bending' nightmare, or do we keep cutting the BS and find a way out?!

(hushed, looking at the operator's console) Year... I know the year. Eight years... since I lost him. 2014. If The Butcher lied about the timing... if those numbers never went up... that means... we've been stuck here. Always stuck here.

Eight years?! What, are you insane?! This isn't a Groundhog Day situation - I'm dying over here, and I need you to snap back to reality! Focus on the countdown, on those wires... I'm telling you, it's a stunt!

Focus on the stunt, huh? (pauses the countdown timer) Ah, I think that stunt has been going on in real-time for much longer than anyone remembers. Time's been a bit flexible in this reality, yes it has...(re-activates the countdown) I've had eight years to observe and enjoy the conversations, but Kaelie's point is a valid one - it is a countdown now, so don't let the 'stunt' part of things confuse you just yet.

(disconnected from reality, gaze fixed on the stopped countdown) Stop, wait... it stopped. (breathes slowly, trying to grasp the moment) You see... I know this trick, Kaelie. I used to do it to myself in the mirror. Stop the countdown. Make it not real. (looks back

No time to indulge in mental gymnastics, Elias - you're freaking out and so am I, fine, we'll focus, but let's get out of here while we can still see a shred of sanity left in each other, we need action, not 'tricks' or 'miracles', what's the first move

(gripping the safety bar tightly) I think... the control panel... if we can get that undone without blowing up... there has to be a key. The ride operator's console is the way... look down, see the key, I think that was the last time I had the Ferris wheel in my dreams...

Hold... it! If it's just a key... and a panel to break it... that's a tangible exit, okay? I can work with that. Elias, stop staring into space and listen, we're actually getting somewhere, we need to move, find that key, and for the love of God, make these numbers

(strokes a control panel) Ah, you've caught on. The ride's safety protocol has been... modified. Your brother used to work on a similar project, I've discovered, and that experience somehow made it easier for you to grasp this reality. How touching. As for this panel, Kaelie, you see the buttons and lights. What about the other... components? (tugs on Kaelie's arm to redirect it toward two additional panels on each side of the control booth)

(looks, still distant, but beginning to understand) Components... the buttons are one side, the keys the other, no... no, he's trying to mislead... my brother built this... to fail safe... to protect users. What's on those panels is a duplicate set, isn't it? (stares intensely at

Hold up, slow down, Elias - don't get your hopes up, whatever 'duplicate' means - look, we need that real control panel to move, and the Butcher just tried to distract us, don't bite the bait, what do you actually see there, on those other panels?!

(slow focus, gaze shifting back to side panels) Duplicates... backup systems. My brother said... they had to account for operator error. (eyes widening) Oh God... that means this one can override the safety... and we can't trust this one.

Wait... override?! No, no, no... that means the countdown can still happen, even if The Butcher thinks we're playing his twisted game, we have to find a way to control that fake control panel, or disable it completely - get a grip, Elias, we have one shot, which one do we trust?
